content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

June 30, 1995

Diane Fishburn
Olson, Hagel, Fong,
Leidigh, Waters and Fishburn
Plaza Towers
555 Capitol Mall, Suite 1425
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-95-210

Dear Ms. Fishburn:

This is to confirm telephone assistance provided to you
regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act.¹ This letter constitutes written advice under Section
84205, which states:

The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit
candidates and committees to file campaign statements
combining statements and reports required to be filed by this
title.

You have inquired about general purpose recipient committees
combining the semi-annual statement with the Form 495,
Supplemental Pre-election Statement in connection with the
upcoming 72nd Assembly District Election to be held on July 18,
1995.

Section 84202.5(c) provides that if a committee makes
contributions totaling five thousand dollars or more in connection
with an election and all of the contributions have been reported
on a semi-annual statement, the committee is not required to file
additional statements for that period.

_______________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5560

Ms. Fishburn
Our File No. 95-210
Page Two


     The filing schedule for the July 18 election allows the
second pre-election statement to be combined with the semi-annual
statement as shown on the schedule below. Therefore, if a general
purpose committee files a statement for the period covering the
day after the closing date of the last statement filed through
July 1 on or before July 6, 1995, a Form 495 Supplemental Pre-
election Statement is not required.


Type of Statement          Period Covered          Filing Deadline

1st Pre-Election           Jan. 1 - June 3'.       June 8

*2nd Pre-Election          June 4 - July 1         July 6

Late Contribution;
  Late Independent
  Expenditure Reports      July 2 - July 17        Within 24 Hours

Semi-Annual                July 2 - Dec. 31        January 31, 1996

*Please note that the second pre-election statement is combined
with the semi-annual statement.


     If you have additional questions, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Dixie Howard
                                        Political Reform Consultant
                                        Technical Assistance Division

cc: Secretary of State, Political Reform Division

sections.question: You have inquired about general purpose recipient committees combining the semi-annual statement with the Form 495, Supplemental Pre-election Statement in connection with the upcoming 72nd Assembly District Election to be held on July 18, 1995.
sections.conclusion: Therefore, if a general purpose committee files a statement for the period covering the day after the closing date of the last statement filed through July 1 on or before July 6, 1995, a Form 495 Supplemental Pre-election Statement is not required.
sections.question_synthetic: May general purpose recipient committees combine the semi-annual statement with the Form 495 Supplemental Pre-election Statement for the July 18, 1995 Assembly District election?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Yes, general purpose committees may combine the second pre-election statement with the semi-annual statement if filed by July 6, 1995, covering the period through July 1. This eliminates the need to file a separate Form 495 Supplemental Pre-election Statement.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format. Question and conclusion are clearly identifiable though not explicitly labeled with headers. Document provides specific filing deadline guidance and a reference table. Minor OCR artifact noted ('FishbUrfl' on page two).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have inquired about general purpose recipient committees combining the semi-annual statement with the Form 495, Supplemental Pre-election Statement in connection with the upcoming 72nd Assembly District Election to be held on July 18, 1995.

CONCLUSION: Therefore, if a general purpose committee files a statement for the period covering the day after the closing date of the last statement filed through July 1 on or before July 6, 1995, a Form 495 Supplemental Pre-election Statement is not required.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms that general purpose recipient committees may combine the semi-annual statement with the Form 495 Supplemental Pre-election Statement for the July 18, 1995 election, provided the combined statement is filed by July 6, 1995. The letter provides a filing schedule showing the permitted consolidation of the second pre-election statement with the semi-annual statement.
